rxn_class_summary: electrochemical 1,2-bifunctionalization of styrenes (and alpha-alkylation of unactivated alkenes) with 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds via anodic radical-polar crossover, installing a C-C bond and a benzoate ester from in situ oxocarbenium ring-opening
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the deprotonated 1,3-dicarbonyl enolate to an alpha-carbon radical; a second anodic oxidation of the benzylic C-radical (after addition to styrene) gives a benzylic cation; cathode reduces water/H+ to H2

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical adduct (III) after radical addition to styrene
benzylic carbocation (IV) after second anodic oxidation
alpha-alkylated alkene product 5aa (from HAT/isomerization with unactivated alkene)

electron_flow: Base (OH- generated cathodically) deprotonates the 1,3-dicarbonyl; the anode oxidizes the enolate to an alpha-keto radical, which adds to the styrene to give a benzylic radical; a second anodic oxidation produces a benzylic cation that is captured intramolecularly by a carbonyl oxygen to form an oxocarbenium, which is opened by hydroxide/water to deliver the benzoate ester product, while H2 evolves at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited; TEMPO-adduct of the 1,3-dicarbonyl radical isolated in 55% and characterized by NMR/HRMS, confirming radical II

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited; BHT-adduct (1a'') detected by HRMS, supporting radical intermediacy

probe_reagents.name: divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3aa formed (60%) only when reaction mixture was in the anodic chamber; no reaction in cathodic chamber, proving anodic initiation

probe_reagents.name: D2O (in place of H2O)
probe_reagents.observation: Product obtained with 99% deuterium incorporation (c1), consistent with protonation of enolate intermediate VII by water

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporated into the benzoate oxygen of the product (c2, HRMS), confirming water as the oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: anhydrous CH3CN under N2
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product formed, indicating water is essential

probe_reagents.name: MeOH co-solvent (trap for oxocarbenium)
probe_reagents.observation: Methoxy-trapped cyclic acetal d2 detected by HRMS, supporting oxocarbenium intermediate V

probe_reagents.name: ethyl 3-oxo-3-phenylpropanoate (1a1) as 1,3-dicarbonyl
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclized gamma-butyrolactone product d1 detected by NMR/HRMS, further supporting the oxocarbenium/cyclization pathway

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a vs 2a
probe_reagents.observation: E_ox(1a) < E_ox(2a) = 1.92 V vs Ag/AgCl, confirming the 1,3-dicarbonyl is oxidized first

notes: With unactivated aliphatic alkenes the benzylic-cation/oxocarbenium pathway is unavailable, so after radical addition the system instead undergoes HAT/double-bond isomerization to give alpha-alkylated alkene products (5a-5d) rather than 1,2-bifunctionalization; benzoic acid is the sole byproduct from collapse of the dibenzoyl-derived oxocarbenium. Synthetic utility includes NaBH4 reduction to gamma-hydroxy esters and KOtBu-mediated conversion of 3aa to trans-cyclopropane 4aa with benzoate as leaving group.
